behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily list/windowing logic at one local boundary (history trimming), not inherently a cross-module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text focuses on trimming rule itself ("trimmed to the most recent 10 messages"), which can be validated directly in the trimming/payload-building function.
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-trimming/payload-builder function with >10 prior messages and assert exactly the latest 10 (correct order) are included in the LLM request payload; include edge cases at 10 and 11 messages.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment/output quality (relevance/off-topic detection target), which stubs would mask. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked response can always be on-topic and miss real failures. MULTI-STEP: single model decision quality, not a multi-agent pipeline, so Agentic not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real LLM with representative customer questions and score whether responses are question-relevant/non-generic using rubric or human-labeled set; fail if relevance rate drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly tests correctness of tone identification ("correctly identifies") for style appropriateness, which depends on model judgment. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic mocks cannot reveal misclassification of tone. MULTI-STEP: this is one core model-evaluation decision plus trigger, not a full multi-agent sequence requiring Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM-generated candidate replies spanning tone spectrum for casual-tier customers; verify tone-check flags overly formal outputs and regeneration path is invoked at acceptable precision/recall thresholds.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundaries (chat orchestration -> persistence layer) and transaction/state handoff across attempts. REAL-INFRA: does not require real external network/OS primitives; a local test DB is sufficient. LLM-DEP: assertions are about persisted records/timestamps, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow with a deterministic fake LLM (including a forced regeneration path) against a real local test DB, then assert all entries exist (user, first draft, regenerated response) with correct linkage and timestamp ordering.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff between tone-check result, regeneration, and response-delivery modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: Agentic considered, but behavior text explicitly scopes to post-trigger replacement semantics ("When the tone check triggers regeneration"), not whether model tone judgments are correct. REJECTION-GATE: higher level rejected because correctness target is delivery-state plumbing, not model-output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service modules with deterministic stubs that force tone-check trigger and two distinct drafts; assert outward-facing response is only the second draft and first draft is inaccessible to customer-facing output while still traceable internally if required.
